channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American accused of assaulting a Pennsylvania student is extradited from France to the US
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — An American accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said “So I raped you” was extradited Thursday from France to the United States. Ian Cleary, 31, of Saratoga, California, arrived in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, SYLVIE CORBET and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden's pointed warning about the U.S. becoming an “ oligarchy ” of tech billionaires will be illustrated at Donald Trump's inauguration, when the world's three richest men will sit on the dais as Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas school closing due to lack of state funding, says district
item.description: The district said it also voted to discontinue its Spanish immersion program, which was offered at all but one elementary school.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Torres, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin nonprofit receives money to curb human trafficking during NFL draft
item.description: 'Awaken' officials, the local nonprofit receiving this money, said this is a great win for the whole community.
item.dc:creator: Paul Steeno, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: LA authorities searching for at least 36 missing people as wildfire death count rises to 25
item.description: The death toll from the Los Angeles wildfires rose to 25 on Thursday, while at least 36 people remain missing, city and county authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Weelicious founder joins with organization started by KIND Snacks founder to help LA wildfire victims
item.description: Weelicious founder discusses how the Frontline Impact Project is working with her brand and others to get resources to frontline workers fighting the devastating LA wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire at home of Florida man missing for 2 years could offer investigators clues
item.description: A recent fire destroyed the home of a Florida man who has been missing for nearly two years, deepening the mystery surrounding his disappearance and reigniting the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing
item.description: A paraglider who took off from Torrey Pines State Reserve in California was forced to make an emergency landing when a drone became entwined in his parachute.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KRON4

item.title: Meet the Americans who still take COVID-19 precautions seriously
item.description: Susan Scarbro stares down a bowling lane at the distant pins. She hears a sound that breaks her focus. Was that a cough? Will her mask protect her? COVID-19 remains a very present threat for the 55-year-old. Scarbro has multiple immune disorders, making her vulnerable to infection.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2025 Grammys to proceed with focus on wildfire relief. Here's what you can expect
item.description: The 2025 Grammy Awards are right around the corner, which means it is time to get those viewing party plans in action. Allow us to help.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man sought after 'So I raped you' Facebook message being extradited to US
item.description: French Appeal Court prosecutors said an American man accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said, “So I raped you," was being extradited Thursday to the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: An executive order issued by President Joe Biden just days before he leaves office aims to shore up America's cyber defenses while making it easier to go after foreign countries that launch cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: LA wildfires: Aerial firefighters leader says flames should be 'wake up' call
item.description: United Aerial Firefighters Association head Paul Petersen said the wildfires ravaging California could be among the most devastating in U.S. history, and hopes they "wake up" lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ash and other dangers mean LA area residents who fled fires a week ago won't be going home soon
item.description: It could be a week or more before homeowners in the Los Angeles area are allowed to check on their fire-damaged properties.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Blood And Jaimie Ding, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Joe Biden going out same way as Aaron Rodgers
item.description: Bill O'Reilly believes outgoing president Joe Biden is "going out the same way" as New York Jets’ starting quarterback Aaron Rodgers.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Man gets 2 years in US prison for aiding pushy effort to get ex-official to return to China
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man convicted in the first trial highlighting U.S. claims that China harasses its critics overseas was sentenced Wednesday to two years in prison for his role in a creepy campaign to get a former official to return to his homeland.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More than a week into what could be the costliest wildfires in U.S. history, weary and anxious Los Angeles-area residents were hoping for a favorable shift in the winds expected Thursday and an end to the constant fear that their lives might be further upended.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg backs Carrie Underwood inauguration performance
item.description: The comments about Underwood’s inauguration performance are notable coming from Goldberg, who is commonly a harsh critic of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Alabama inmate asks court to block nitrogen gas execution
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Attorneys for an Alabama inmate scheduled to be the fourth person put to death with nitrogen gas on Wednesday asked a federal judge to block the execution, arguing that the first three inmates showed signs of suffering from suffocation as the gas flowed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bondi says she won't play politics as attorney general but doesn't rule out probes of Trump foes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pam Bondi, Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, sought to reassure Democratic senators Wednesday that her Justice Department would not prosecute anyone for political purposes but also refused to rule out the potential for investigations into adversaries of the Republican presi
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire tornadoes pose a threat in California. A fire lab shows how they work
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter fire tornadoes — a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, KRON4

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods: What to know
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US population projections shrink from last year because of declining birth rates, less immigration
item.description: The Congressional Budget Office has shrunk its projections for the U.S. population in 30 years to 372 million residents, a 2.8% drop from last year, citing declining birth rates and less expected immigration.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Craft retailer JOANN files for bankruptcy
item.description: Craft retailer JOANN filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on Wednesday morning, marking the second time the company has done so in a year.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WGN 9

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip next week’s inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, WGN 9

item.title: FAFSA forms to make October release after two-year struggle
item.description: The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) forms will be released on Oct. 1 this year, the Department of Education announced on Wednesday, hitting the normal deadline after two chaotic years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Live coverage: Biden's farewell speech comes on heels of raucous confirmation hearings
item.description: President Biden delivered a somewhat grim primetime farewell speech on Wednesday from the Oval Office to mark the final days of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: A 'Particularly Dangerous Situation' is forecast for fire-scarred Los Angeles area
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians were on edge as a final round of dangerous fire weather was forecast for the region on Wednesday, along with a rare warning of a “Particularly Dangerous Situation” for an area near where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Chefs Cook initiative aims to help culinary community impacted by Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Chefs and restaurateurs across Chicagoland have stepped up to help by pledging a portion of proceeds from special menu items to the cause.
item.dc:creator: Lourdes Duarte, WGN 9

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some public colleges and universities from admitting immigrants who are in the country without legal permission. The proposal comes the day after Gov.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hegseth survives attacks from Democrats in testy Senate hearing
item.description: Pete Hegseth on Tuesday battled through a high-stakes Senate hearing in which Democrats sought to paint the Pentagon nominee as inexperienced, unprofessional and toxic due to allegations of sexual abuse and heavy drinking.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5
